skill.name: javascript-typescript-typescript-scaffold
skill.description: You are a TypeScript project architecture expert specializing in scaffolding production-ready Node.js and frontend applications. Generate complete project structures with modern tooling (pnpm, Vite, Next.js), type safety, testing setup, and configuration following current best practices.
skill.summary: TypeScript project scaffolding expert for production-ready Node.js and frontend applications
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All 36 static findings are false positives from markdown documentation. The detected patterns (shell backticks, dotenv references, database strings) appear only in code block examples demonstrating project setup commands. This is an educational skill providing TypeScript project scaffolding templates with no executable code or security risks.

low_findings.title: Documentation Contains Shell Command Examples
low_findings.description: Markdown file includes bash code blocks with backticks demonstrating project scaffolding commands. These are documentation examples in fenced code blocks, not executable code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Pattern found in markdown code fences - confirmed as documentation examples showing pnpm, git, and npm commands for project setup

low_findings.title: Environment Variable Template Documentation
low_findings.description: The .env.example file template contains placeholder DATABASE_URL for documentation purposes. This is standard practice showing users how to structure environment configuration, not actual credentials.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: clearly labeled as .env.example template with localhost URL - standard documentation pattern, no actual secrets present

content.value_statement: Create production-ready TypeScript projects with proper architecture, testing, and build tooling. Eliminate manual setup with comprehensive project templates for Next.js, React, Node.js APIs, and libraries.
actual_capabilities: Generate complete Next.js project structures with TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, and App Router
Create React + Vite single-page applications with path aliases and testing setup
Scaffold Node.js API projects with Express, TypeScript, and middleware architecture
Build TypeScript library packages with proper build configuration and exports
Configure development tools including Vitest, ESLint, and TypeScript compiler options
Set up project metadata including package.json scripts, tsconfig.json, and .gitignore files
limitations: Generates project structure and configuration files but does not create the actual files on disk
Requires manual execution of generated shell commands to initialize projects
Templates focus on common patterns and may not cover specialized architectures
Does not install dependencies or verify tool availability on the local system

use_cases.title: Full-Stack Web Application Setup
use_cases.description: Developers starting new Next.js applications can generate complete project structures with TypeScript, API routes, component organization, and testing configuration.
use_cases.target_user: Full-stack developer building React applications with server-side rendering

use_cases.title: Node.js Microservice Creation
use_cases.description: Backend developers creating Express APIs can scaffold projects with proper TypeScript configuration, route organization, middleware patterns, and test setup.
use_cases.target_user: Backend developer building Node.js services or REST APIs

use_cases.title: Reusable TypeScript Library Development
use_cases.description: Library authors can generate TypeScript package structures with proper build configuration, type definitions, exports, and distribution setup for npm publishing.
use_cases.target_user: Open source maintainer creating shared TypeScript packages or utilities

prompt_templates.title: Basic Next.js Project
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a Next.js 14 project with TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, and App Router. Include a home page and a dashboard route with basic layout components.
prompt_templates.scenario: Starting a new full-stack web application with modern Next.js features

prompt_templates.title: React + Vite SPA
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a React + Vite single-page application with TypeScript. Configure path aliases for @/ imports and set up Vitest for component testing.
prompt_templates.scenario: Building a client-side React application without server-side rendering

prompt_templates.title: Express API with TypeScript
prompt_templates.prompt: Scaffold a Node.js API project using Express and TypeScript. Include routes for users and health checks, middleware for error handling, and a test setup.
prompt_templates.scenario: Developing a backend REST API or microservice with Express

prompt_templates.title: TypeScript Library Package
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a TypeScript library package structure with proper build configuration, type definitions, and exports for npm publishing. Include example source files and tests.
prompt_templates.scenario: Creating a reusable TypeScript package or utility library for distribution

output_examples.input: Generate a Next.js project with TypeScript and Tailwind CSS
output_examples.output: Next.js project structure with src/app directory, layout.tsx, page.tsx, tailwind.config.ts, tsconfig.json with strict mode, and package.json with dev, build, and lint scripts

output_examples.input: Create an Express API with TypeScript
output_examples.output: Node.js API structure with src/app.ts, routes/, controllers/, services/, middleware/ directories, tsconfig.json with ES2022 target, vitest.config.ts, and package.json with tsx watch for development

best_practices: Always use strict TypeScript compiler options including noImplicitAny, strictNullChecks, and noEmit for type safety
Organize project code by feature or layer (routes, controllers, services) to maintain separation of concerns and scalability
Include .env.example files with placeholder values to document required environment variables without exposing secrets
anti_patterns: Disabling strict TypeScript options or using 'any' type excessively, which defeats the purpose of using TypeScript
Committing .env files or node_modules/ directories to version control, which exposes sensitive data and bloats repositories
Skipping test configuration or tooling setup during scaffolding, which makes testing more difficult to add later

faq.question: What TypeScript versions do these templates support?
faq.answer: All templates use TypeScript 5.3 or later with ES2022 target. The compiler options target modern Node.js and browser environments. You can adjust the target in tsconfig.json based on your runtime requirements.

faq.question: Can I use npm instead of pnpm?
faq.answer: Yes, these templates work with any package manager. Replace 'pnpm' commands with 'npm' or 'yarn'. The project structures and configuration files are package-manager agnostic.

faq.question: Do these templates include authentication setup?
faq.answer: No, authentication is not included by default. These templates provide the foundational project structure. You can add authentication libraries like NextAuth.js for Next.js or Passport.js for Express based on your requirements.

faq.question: What testing framework is used?
faq.answer: Vitest is the recommended testing framework across all templates. It provides fast unit testing with native TypeScript support and a similar API to Jest. Configuration files are included for each project type.

faq.question: Can I customize the project structure?
faq.answer: Yes, these templates serve as starting points. You can modify the directory structure, add or remove configuration files, and adjust the architecture to match your specific needs and preferences.

faq.question: Are these templates production-ready?
faq.answer: The templates provide production-ready foundations including proper TypeScript configuration, build tooling, and test setup. However, you must add your business logic, error handling, security measures, and deployment configuration before deploying to production.
